description: Full-featured portfolio website with Supabase backend, Cloudinary media hosting, and Firebase analytics
lessonsLearned:
# Lessons Learned from Initial Setup

## Tech Stack
- Framework: Next.js 16 with App Router and React 19
- Package Manager: pnpm
- UI: shadcn/ui with Radix UI components
- Styling: Tailwind CSS v4 with custom animations
- Analytics: Vercel Analytics already integrated
- 3D: @react-three/fiber available for potential 3D elements

## Architecture
- Project structure: app/, components/, data/, hooks/, lib/, types/
- Server Components + Client Components pattern
- RSC-based data fetching approach

## Notes
- No existing PRDs found - this is the initial feature
- This feature establishes foundational patterns for future features

## MCP Status
- No MCP servers currently configured in environment
- PRD includes MCP assignments for when servers are added

userStories.title: Supabase Database Schema & Client Setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to set up the Supabase database schema and client configuration so that the portfolio can store and retrieve dynamic content.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .env.local file with Supabase credentials (project ID: njcggtsozmjhvladuznw)
Set up Supabase client in lib/supabase/client.ts for client components
Set up Supabase server client in lib/supabase/server.ts for RSC
Create projects table with id, title, description, slug, tags, images, demo_url, repo_url, featured, created_at, updated_at, published_at
Create posts table with id, title, slug, content, excerpt, cover_image, tags, published, created_at, updated_at, published_at
Create contacts table with id, name, email, message, created_at, read
Create TypeScript types from database schema
userStories.notes: NOTE: supabase MCP not currently configured - add when available

userStories.title: Cloudinary Media Integration
userStories.description: As a content creator, I want to upload and manage images through Cloudinary so that media is optimized and delivered efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Cloudinary credentials to .env.local (cloud_name: dszboz3se, api_key: 293868987781493)
Create components/media-upload.tsx with Cloudinary upload widget
Add helper functions for image transformations (resize, crop, format)

userStories.title: Dynamic Projects Display
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to browse projects dynamically loaded from the database so that new projects can be added without code changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/projects/[slug]/page.tsx for individual project pages
Fetch projects from Supabase in server components
Implement pagination or infinite scroll for many projects
SEO metadata for each project page
userStories.notes: NOTE: supabase MCP not currently configured - add when available

userStories.title: Blog System with Markdown Support
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to read blog posts stored in the database so that content can be managed dynamically.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/blog/page.tsx listing all published posts
Store post content as Markdown in Supabase
Implement Markdown rendering with syntax highlighting
userStories.notes: Step 3 complete - Feature-based structure verified. Blog follows same pattern as projects feature.

userStories.title: Contact Form with Database Storage
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to submit a contact form so that I can reach out to the portfolio owner.
acceptanceCriteria: Create contact form component with validation (Zod schema)
Store submissions in Supabase contacts table
Implement rate limiting to prevent spam
Add success/error feedback with toast notifications
Create admin view to read contact submissions (optional authentication)
userStories.notes: Implementation complete: Contact form with Zod validation, rate limiting (5 requests per hour), Supabase database storage, toast notifications, input sanitization, and admin view for reading submissions. All security measures implemented including CSRF protection and XSS prevention.

userStories.title: Firebase Analytics Integration
userStories.description: As a site owner, I want to track visitor behavior through Firebase Analytics so that I can understand user engagement.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement consent banner for GDPR compliance
userStories.notes: Implementation complete: Firebase Analytics integrated with SDK (firebase v12.9.0), initialized in lib/firebase.ts with all required configuration. Page view tracking implemented via AnalyticsProvider with automatic route change detection. Event tracking added for project clicks, blog post views, contact form submissions, external link clicks, and search queries. GDPR-compliant consent banner implemented with localStorage persistence. Build passes successfully with no type errors in analytics implementation.

userStories.title: Real-time Content Updates
userStories.description: As a content manager, I want to see content updates immediately without refreshing so that I can preview changes in real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: Enable Supabase real-time on projects and posts tables
Implement real-time subscriptions for project list
Implement real-time subscriptions for blog list
userStories.notes: Implementation complete: Real-time subscriptions implemented for both projects and posts tables using Supabase real-time. Created hooks/use-realtime-projects.ts and hooks/use-realtime-posts.ts with connection state management (connecting, connected, disconnected, error), throttled updates (500ms default), and automatic cleanup. Added components/connection-status.tsx with visual indicators showing live connection status and last update time. Implemented lib/utils/debounce.ts with debounce, debounceAsync, and throttle functions. Migration 002_enable_realtime.sql added to enable real-time on Supabase tables. Both /projects and /blog pages updated with real-time wrappers. Build passes successfully.

userStories.title: SEO Optimization & Metadata
userStories.description: As a site owner, I want proper SEO so that the portfolio ranks well in search engines.
acceptanceCriteria: Open Graph tags for social sharing
userStories.notes: Implementation complete: SEO structured data (JSON-LD) added for all pages. Created lib/seo/json-ld.tsx with components for Organization, Person, WebSite, WebPage, Article, CreativeWork, BreadcrumbList, CollectionPage, and ProfilePage schemas. Added dynamic sitemap.ts fetching projects and posts from Supabase. Added canonical URLs via alternates.canonical in metadata. Created components/seo/breadcrumb.tsx with JSON-LD structured data support. Homepage includes PersonSchema and WebSiteSchema. Project pages use CreativeWorkSchema. Blog posts use ArticleSchema. Listing pages use CollectionPageSchema. Breadcrumb navigation added to all main pages.

userStories.title: Content Management Interface (Optional Admin)
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a simple interface to manage content so that I don't need to use the Supabase dashboard directly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create post CRUD interface with Markdown editor
Add image upload integration with Cloudinary
userStories.notes: Implementation complete: Admin interface created with layout at app/admin/layout.tsx with password protection using ADMIN_PASSWORD environment variable. Project CRUD interface at app/admin/projects/page.tsx and app/admin/projects/[id]/page.tsx. Post CRUD interface at app/admin/posts/page.tsx and app/admin/posts/[id]/page.tsx with Markdown editor. Image upload integration with Cloudinary via components/admin/image-upload.tsx. Draft/published status management implemented. Bulk actions (delete, publish) added. Content preview mode available. Contact submissions view at app/admin/contact/page.tsx. Build passes successfully.
